sanskrit: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति । वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  हे कृष्ण युद्ध की इच्छावाले इस कुटुम्बसमुदाय को अपने सामने उपस्थित देखकर मेरे अङ्ग शिथिल हो रहे हैं और मुख सूख रहा है तथा मेरे शरीर में कँपकँपी आ रही है एवं रोंगटे खड़े हो रहे हैं ।  हाथ से गाण्डीव धनुष गिर रहा है और त्वचा भी जल रही है ।  मेरा मन भ्रमितसा हो रहा है और मैं खड़े रहने में भी असमर्थ हो रहा हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: My limbs are failing, my mouth is drying up, my body trembles, and my hair stands on end.
essence: The body reveals fear before the mind can explain it.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna's body is breaking under the pressure of what he sees. His strength, speech, and composure are all slipping away at once.
simpleInsight: Overwhelm shows up in the body before it becomes a decision.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands before his own relatives and teachers. Seeing them in the opposing army, his body begins to fail: his limbs weaken, his mouth dries, and fear shakes him. This verse deepens his collapse before Krishna answers.
modernRelevance: You open the message you were dreading, and your hands go cold before you even reply. The body often knows the cost of a hard truth first.
translationLiteral: सीदन्ति (are failing) / मम (my) / गात्राणि (limbs) / मुखं (mouth) / च (and) / परिशुष्यति (dries up) / वेपथुः (trembling) / च (and) / शरीरे (in the body) / मे (my) / रोम-हर्षः (standing of hair) / च (and) / जायते (arises)
insight: It teaches you to notice distress as a physical signal, not just a mental opinion.
